title: Arrays
subtitle: One box, many values — keep a whole list of things in a single variable.
goal: Make arrays, read items by their position, find the length, loop over every value, and tidy them up with the Arrays helper.

blocks.heading: What is an array? 📊
blocks.html:
So far each variable held just one thing: one number, one name. But what if you have the scores of a whole class? You don't want 30 variables!

An array is one variable that holds many values of the same type — like an egg carton with a slot for each egg. All ints, or all Strings, lined up in a row.

blocks.heading: Making an array
blocks.html:
Two common ways. Fill it right away with values in curly braces:

int[] nums = {1, 2, 3};

Or make an empty one of a fixed size (Java fills it with zeros for you):

int[] five = new int[5];

The [] means "this is an array of that type."

blocks.caption: Reading items and counting them
blocks.explain: Counting starts at ZERO. scores[0] is the first item (90), scores[1] is the second. The .length tells you how many items are inside — notice there are no parentheses after length.

blocks.html: Zero is the start! The first slot is [0], not [1]. So an array with 3 items uses slots [0], [1], and [2].

blocks.caption: Two ways to loop over an array
blocks.output:
Hello, Mia!
Hello, Leo!
Hello, Zoe!
Mia is here.
Leo is here.
Zoe is here.
blocks.explain: The first loop uses a counter i to grab names[i]. The second is a 'for-each': read it as 'for each name in names'. The for-each is simpler when you just want every item in order.

blocks.html: Watch the edge! If an array has 3 items, the last slot is [2]. Asking for scores[3] reaches past the end and Java throws an ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException. Always stop at length - 1.

blocks.caption: The Arrays toolbox: toString and sort
blocks.explain: You must write import java.util.Arrays; at the top to use these. Arrays.toString() makes an array print nicely. Arrays.sort() puts the numbers in order from smallest to biggest — for free!

blocks.heading: A peek at 2D arrays 🗂️
blocks.html:
An array can even hold other arrays — like a grid, or a tic-tac-toe board. We call this a 2D array.

int[][] grid = {{1, 2, 3}, {4, 5, 6}};

Now grid[0] is the first row, and grid[0][1] is the second item of that row (which is 2). You'll meet these more later — for now, just know rows of rows are possible!

blocks.question: In int[] a = {10, 20, 30}; what is a[1]?
blocks.explain: Counting starts at 0, so a[0] is 10 and a[1] is 20.

blocks.question: An array has 4 items. What happens if you ask for item [4]?
options: It wraps around to the first item
blocks.explain: With 4 items the slots are [0], [1], [2], [3]. Asking for [4] reaches past the end, so Java throws an ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException.

blocks.question: Which method puts an int array in order from smallest to biggest?
blocks.explain: Arrays.sort() sorts the array in place. Remember to import java.util.Arrays; first.

blocks.title: Grab the third score
blocks.instructions: The array has four scores. Print the THIRD score. Remember: counting starts at 0, so the third item lives at index 2!
blocks.passMsg: Nice — you remembered that the third item is index 2!
blocks.failMsg: The third score is at index 2 (because counting starts at 0). Use scores[2].

blocks.title: Find the average
blocks.instructions: Loop over the array, add up every number into total, then divide by how many there are. Fill in the blanks so it prints the average.
blocks.passMsg: You averaged a whole array — that's real data work!
blocks.failMsg: To divide by the count of items, use nums.length.

challenge.title: Print a number grid
challenge.instructions: Use a 2D array to make a little grid. Loop over each row, then each column inside that row, and print the numbers with a space after each. Print a new line after every row.
challenge.hint: Inside the row loop, after the column loop finishes, call System.out.println() with nothing inside to drop to the next line.
challenge.passMsg: 🏆 A whole grid from arrays inside arrays — Day 9 conquered. You're handling real data now!
